name: Embalse de Cernadilla y Sierra de la Culebra — circular por Puebla de Sanabria
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Manzanal De Arriba traverse a diverse landscape characterized by the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de la Culebra, featuring winding roads through oak, chestnut, and pine forests. The region includes significant natural features such as the Embalse de Valparaíso and Embalse de Cernadilla reservoirs, offering scenic routes alongside water bodies. Cyclists can expect varied elevation changes across this part of the Zamora province. Best road cycling routes around Manzanal De Arriba The most popular road cycling route is Palacios de Sanabria – tour around Eriá river and Truchas – Alto Peñón, a 66.1 miles (106.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours 13 minutes to complete. This challenging route features significant elevation gain, passing through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Valparaíso and Cernadilla Reservoirs Loop, a moderate 36.6 miles (58.9 km) path. This route offers views of the Valparaíso and Cernadilla reservoirs, providing a mix of open and forested scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Embalse de Cernadilla y Sierra de la Culebra — circular por Puebla de Sanabria, a 49.4 miles (79.5 km) trail leading through the Sierra de la Culebra, often completed in about 3 hours 58 minutes. Road cycling around Manzanal De Arriba is defined by mountainous terrain, dense oak and chestnut forests, and scenic reservoirs. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Manzanal De Arriba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Manzanal De Arriba?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes available around Manzanal De Arriba, offering a diverse range of experiences through the region's natural landscapes. These routes cater to both moderate and difficult ability levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Manzanal De Arriba?
A: The region's oak and chestnut forests offer stunning colors throughout the seasons, making spring and autumn particularly picturesque for road cycling. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially for routes that pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1679368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Valparaíso&lt;/a&gt;, where you can cool off at its crystalline beach.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation in the area?
A: Yes, Manzanal De Arriba, situated in the mountainous Sierra de la Culebra, offers several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1024435001/palacios-de-sanabria-tour-around-eria-river-and-truchas-alto-penon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palacios de Sanabria – tour around Eriá river and Truchas – Alto Peñón&lt;/a&gt; route is a difficult 66.1-mile (106.4 km) trail with significant elevation gain, taking you through varied landscapes. The highest point in the Sierra de la Culebra, Peña Mira (1243 meters), also provides challenging climbs.

Q: Can I find moderate road cycling routes suitable for a less intense ride?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer a moderate challenge, perfect for enjoying the scenery without extreme difficulty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1024587428/valparaiso-and-cernadilla-reservoirs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valparaíso and Cernadilla Reservoirs Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 36.6-mile (58.9 km) path, offering beautiful views of the reservoirs and a mix of open and forested scenery.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Manzanal De Arriba?
A: Yes, the region features several circular routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1368839991/embalse-de-cernadilla-y-sierra-de-la-culebra-circular-por-puebla-de-sanabria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Cernadilla y Sierra de la Culebra — circular por Puebla de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;, a 49.4-mile (79.5 km) trail that leads through the Sierra de la Culebra, offering a comprehensive tour of the area's natural beauty.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Manzanal De Arriba often pass through the rich oak and chestnut forests of the Sierra de la Culebra. You can also enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1679368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Valparaíso&lt;/a&gt; and Embalse de Cernadilla reservoirs. The picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1679368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio de Onor Village&lt;/a&gt; and its magical views from the Ponte de Rio de Onor are also accessible on some routes.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical attractions near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond natural beauty, the region offers several cultural stops. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3866268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Incarnation&lt;/a&gt; in Palacios de Sanabria, various historic stone churches, or even the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5539760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ethnographic Museum in Codesal&lt;/a&gt;. The traditional architecture of stone houses in the villages adds to the charm.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Manzanal De Arriba?
A: Parking is generally available in the villages and near popular starting points for routes. For instance, routes originating from the Embalse de Valparaíso area, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1024562485/muelas-de-los-caballeros-from-embalse-de-valparaiso-circular-via-peque-and-mombuey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muelas de los Caballeros from Embalse de Valparaíso – circular via Peque and Mombuey&lt;/a&gt;, typically have designated parking areas nearby.

Q: Are there cafes or pubs along the road cycling routes for a break?
A: While specific cafes and pubs directly on every route can vary, the villages within the Manzanal De Arriba municipality and surrounding areas often have local establishments where cyclists can stop for refreshments. Planning your route to pass through these villages will allow for convenient breaks.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Manzanal De Arriba?
A: Road cyclists frequently praise the region's diverse and well-preserved natural landscapes, particularly the winding roads through deep forests of oaks, chestnuts, and pines in the Sierra de la Culebra. The scenic views of the Embalse de Valparaíso and the tranquility of the area are also highly appreciated.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de la Culebra provides numerous opportunities for panoramic views. The highest point, Peña Mira, offers expansive vistas. Additionally, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025465454/sierra-de-la-culebra-loop-from-mahide" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Culebra Loop from Mahide&lt;/a&gt; will immerse you in the region's elevated landscapes, often revealing stunning viewpoints.
tips: One of the two reservoirs with hydroelectric dams on the Tera river in the Sanabria valley in the province of Zamora. Lots of nice views of the reservoirs with places to rest and swim along the lakes and river.
A beautiful little catholic church in Palacios de Sanabria. There is parking nearby and a few cafes if you want to get some food.
Small town in the Sanabria region on the N-525 Otero de Sanabria road with typical houses built of stone and slate. The most notable sight of the town is a Sanctuary of Our Lady of Remedies.

One of the most beautiful towns in the Zamora region in Castilla y León. It was declared a protected historical-artistic complex, with the 15th century fortified castle being one of the many monuments that the city offers.
Picturesque town on the banks of the Onar river. The views from the Ponte de Rio de Onor are magical.
A small town in the Aliste region, Riomanzanas is located a few kilometers from the Sierra de la Culebra. The town is close to the border with Portugal.

Ascent by bicycle to the El Portillón pass in the Sierra de la Culebra. From the top 1068m the views are spectacular. At 7.22km long and with an average gradient of 5.8%, it's not an easy climb. Go at your pace.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Our Lady of Remedies, Otero de Sanabria
Puerto de Peña Mira Ascent, Sierra de la Culebra
